title: pyrus × torminaloides
intro: Pyrus × torminaloides, also known as the Wild Service Tree, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and Western Asia, resulting from a hybrid between Pyrus communis and Sorbus torminalis. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including their astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pyrus × torminaloides include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of the Wild Service Tree typically involve making a tea from its bark and leaves, or using it in tincture form to harness its medicinal properties.
